stem: A function \( f \) satisfies \( f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y) \) for all real numbers \( x \) and \( y \). If \( f([[p]]) = [[fp]] \), what is \( f([[q]]) \)?
solution: Additivity forces \( f \) to be linear through the origin, so \( f(x) = \dfrac{f([[p]])}{[[p]]}\,x = [[a]]x \). Hence \( f([[q]]) = ([[a]])([[q]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This treats the growth rate beyond \( [[p]] \) as 1 instead of \( [[a]] \).
This adds \( [[q]] \) to \( f([[p]]) \) rather than scaling correctly.
This computes \( f([[p]] + [[q]]) \) instead of \( f([[q]]) \).
This multiplies \( f([[p]]) \) by \( [[q]] \), misapplying the scaling rule.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: A function \( f \) satisfies \( f(x + y) = f(x)f(y) \) for all real numbers \( x \) and \( y \), and \( f(1) = [[a]] \). What is \( f([[n]]) \)?
solution: Writing \( [[n]] = 1+1+\cdots+1 \) ([[n]] times) and applying the equation repeatedly gives \( f([[n]]) = f(1)^{[[n]]} = ([[a]])^{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This multiplies \( f(1) \) by [[n]] instead of raising it to the [[n]]-th power, confusing the additive and multiplicative rules.
This uses one less than the correct exponent.
This uses one more than the correct exponent.
This mixes a linear factor of [[n]] with an incorrect fixed power of 2.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: A function \( f \) satisfies \( f(xy) = f(x) + f(y) \) for all positive real numbers \( x \) and \( y \). If \( f([[p]]) = [[A]] \) and \( f([[q]]) = [[B]] \), what is \( f([[pq]]) \)?
solution: Setting \( x = [[p]] \) and \( y = [[q]] \) directly in the functional equation gives \( f([[pq]]) = f([[p]]) + f([[q]]) = ([[A]]) + ([[B]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This multiplies the two given values instead of adding them, confusing this rule with a multiplicative functional equation.
This subtracts \( f([[q]]) \) instead of adding it.
This subtracts \( f([[p]]) \) instead of adding it (subtraction in the wrong order).
This divides the two given values, which has no basis in the functional equation.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: A function \( f \) satisfies \( f(xy) = f(x)f(y) \) for all positive real numbers \( x \) and \( y \). If \( f([[p]]) = [[A]] \), what is \( f([[pn]]) \)? (Note \( [[pn]] = [[p]]^{[[n]]} \).)
solution: Since \( [[pn]] = [[p]]^{[[n]]} = [[p]]\cdot[[p]]\cdots[[p]] \) ([[n]] factors), repeated use of the equation gives \( f([[pn]]) = f([[p]])^{[[n]]} = ([[A]])^{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This multiplies \( f([[p]]) \) by [[n]] instead of raising it to the [[n]]-th power.
This uses one less than the correct exponent.
This uses one more than the correct exponent.
This scales linearly by [[n]] instead of applying the multiplicative rule repeatedly.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: A function \( f \) satisfies \( f(x + [[c]]) = f(x) + ([[d]]) \) for all real \( x \). If \( f([[x0]]) = [[v0]] \), what is \( f([[target]]) \)?
solution: Each step of size [[c]] in \( x \) adds ([[d]]) to the output. Going from [[x0]] to [[target]] takes [[n]] such steps, so \( f([[target]]) = f([[x0]]) + [[n]]\cdot([[d]]) = ([[v0]]) + [[n]]\cdot([[d]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the drift ([[d]]) only once instead of [[n]] times.
This multiplies by the drift instead of adding it [[n]] times.
This adds [[n]] copies of the step size [[c]] instead of the drift ([[d]]).
This subtracts the accumulated drift instead of adding it.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: A function \( f \) satisfies \( f(x) + f([[a]] - x) = [[c]] \) for all real \( x \). What is \( f([[half]]) \)?
solution: Setting \( x = [[half]] \) makes the two arguments equal: \( f([[half]]) + f([[a]] - [[half]]) = 2f([[half]]) = [[c]] \), so \( f([[half]]) = \dfrac{[[c]]}{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to divide by 2 after setting the two arguments equal.
This doubles the constant instead of halving it.
This divides by \( [[a]] \) instead of by 2.
This negates the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: A function \( f \) defined on the positive integers satisfies \( f(n) = f(n-1) + f(n-2) \) for every integer \( n \ge 3 \), with \( f(1) = [[a]] \) and \( f(2) = [[b]] \). What is \( f(6) \)?
distractor_rationales: This stops at \( f(3) \) instead of continuing the recurrence to \( f(6) \).
This stops at \( f(5) \) instead of continuing one more step to \( f(6) \).
This swaps the coefficients of \( f(1) \) and \( f(2) \) in the correct combination.
This treats \( f(6) \) as a fixed multiple of \( f(1)+f(2) \) rather than following the actual Fibonacci-style coefficients.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
